revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Yamato Takeru]]
[[Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient burial mound, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' * The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. * In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': * '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) * '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) * '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya * '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)}} Shrine|ja|葛飾神社}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Shrine|ja|椋橋総社}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社}} * {{ill|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Yamato Takeru]]
[[Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient burial mound, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' * The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. * In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': * '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) * '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) * '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya * '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)}} In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient burial mound, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' * The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. * In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': * '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) * '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) * '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya * '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)}} provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Yamato Takeru]]
[[Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient burial mound, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' * The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. * In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': * '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) * '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) * '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya * '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)}} {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Shrine|ja|椋橋総社}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社}} * {{ill|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} City)-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Tachibana Shrine]] [[Category:Tachibana Shrine]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Yamato Takeru]]
[[Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient burial mound, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' * The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. * In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': * '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) * '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) * '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya * '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)}} {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Shrine|ja|椋橋総社}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社}} * {{ill|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Yamato Takeru]]
[[Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient burial mound, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' * The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. * In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': * '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) * '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) * '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya * '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)}} {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社}} * {{ill|Katsushika {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社}} * {{ill|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} [[Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient burial mound, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' * The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. * In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': * '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) * '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient burial mound, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' * The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. * In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': * '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) * '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) * '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya * '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Yamato Takeru]]
[[Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient burial mound, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' * The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. * In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': * '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) * '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) * '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya * '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)}} [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Ototachibana-hime]] [[Category:Sōja shrines in Japan]] == Here is a list of provincial soja including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Uenoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Shrine|ja|椋橋総社}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社}} * {{ill|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:Regional Sōja|ja|Category:総社 (地域)}} [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Tachibana clan]] {{Sōja_shrines}}[[Category:橘樹神社 (川崎市)]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "burial mound" to "Kofun"
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Yamato Takeru]]
[[Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' * The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. * In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': * '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) * '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) * '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya * '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)}} [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Ototachibana-hime]] [[Category:Sōja shrines in Japan]] == Here is a list of provincial soja including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Uenoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Shrine|ja|椋橋総社}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社}} * {{ill|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Yamato Takeru]]
[[Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' * [[Yamato Takeru]] * [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' * The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. * In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': * '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) * '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) * '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya * '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station * '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Historical sites in Takatsu Ward]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Ototachibana-hime]] [[Category:Sōja shrines in Japan]] == Here is a list of provincial soja including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Uenoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Shrine|ja|椋橋総社}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社}} * {{ill|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| {{ill|Category:Regional Sōja|ja|Category:総社 (地域)}} [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Tachibana clan]] {{Sōja_shrines}}[[Category:橘樹神社 (川崎市)]] City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)}}
