{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Yamato Takeru]]
[[Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)}} {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Shrine|ja|椋橋総社}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社}} * {{ill|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Yamato Takeru]]
[[Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja (Shinto)|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Yamato Takeru]]
[[Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Shrines in Kawasaki, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Historical sites in Takatsu Ward]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Ototachibana-hime]] [[Category:Sōja shrines in Japan]] == Here is a list of provincial soja including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Uenoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Shrine|ja|椋橋総社}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社}} * {{ill|Sazama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社}} * {{ill|Nagao Shrine|ja|長尾神社}} * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Arayaza Amaterasu Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社}} * {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|simple}} * {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|ja|鷲宮神社}} It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki)}} revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=橘樹神社 | 画像=[[画像:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | 所在地=神奈川県川崎市高津区子母口122 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=日本武尊、弟橘媛 | 社格=村社 | 創建=不詳 | 例祭=10月8日 | 地図={{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=橘樹神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''橘樹神社'''（たちばなじんじゃ）は[[神奈川県]][[川崎市]][[高津区]][[子母口]]（旧[[武蔵国]][[橘樹郡]][[橘樹郷]][[子母口村]]、[[橘村 (神奈川県)|橘村]]{{small|大字}}子母口）にある[[神社]]。[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]、[[弟橘媛]]を祀る。[[明治]]以降の[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度#諸社|村社]]。近隣に橘樹[[郡衙]]が置かれていたと推定される地域にあることから、かつて橘樹郡の[[総社]]であったと考えられている<ref>[[川崎歴史ガイド#橘樹郡家と影向寺|川崎歴史ガイド「橘樹郡家と]][[影向寺]]」、川崎市文化財団、2004年（平成16年）</ref>。
==由緒== 古くからの子母口村の[[鎮守]]で、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]と[[弟橘媛]]の男女2躯の神体を祀り、かつては'''立花社'''ともいわれていた。
社伝によると「日本武尊東征の際海が荒れ、弟橘媛はその身を投じ海を鎮た。やがて入水した媛の御衣・御冠の具だけがこの地に漂着した。」とある。また[[古事記]]でも「かれ七日ありて後に、其の后の御櫛海辺によりたりき。すなわち、その櫛を取りて御陵を作りて治め置きき」と伝えられている。
この社伝と古事記の記述とが結びつき、近くの子母口富士見台の高台にある「富士見台古墳」は、一説には弟橘媛の「御陵」であるとも伝えられているが、史料がなく具体的なことは分かっていない<ref>[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html 橘樹神社と富士見台古墳（川崎市教育委員会文化財課）]</ref>。
===富士見台古墳=== 橘樹神社の裏手の丘にあり、前述のとおり[[弟橘媛]]陵であるとする説がある一方、この[[古墳]]は[[6世紀]]頃に造られたもので、当時のこの地域の有力者の墓であるとする説もある<ref>[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm プラザ高津 - 高津の地名探訪 - 富士見台] </ref>。
ここ富士見台は[[江戸時代]]までは旧子母口村の一角であったが、近年になり都市化が進むと[[川崎市]]が本古墳周辺に宅地を造成し、地名も「子母口富士見台」に改められている。
かつては[[多摩川]]沿いの[[田|田園]]風景の中にそびえる丘であった富士見台は、現在は宅地造成や道路敷設によって削られ、古墳頂上部の一部、高さ 3.7[[メートル|m]]、直径 17.5m の部分のみが姿を留めており、公園として管理されている。頂上に登れるよう綱が渡されているが、その頂上から見える眺望は近傍に並ぶ住宅に遮られている。
現在の古墳頂上からは眺望は広がっていないが、古墳がある丘陵地（富士見台）の西側は[[矢上川]]へ落ちる斜面となり、[[富士山]]を眺望でき、東側の斜面からは眼下に[[多摩川低地|多摩川の沖積低地]]が広がり（現在は住宅街が広がる）、子母口公園からは眺望が広がる。南側斜面からは[[蟹ヶ谷]]・[[井田山]]の丘陵の眺望が広がる（[[たちばなの散歩道]]）。
==祭神== *主祭神：[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]、[[弟橘媛]]
== 主な年中行事 == * 例祭：（10月8日）
==文化財== * 橘樹神社社殿：創建年代は不明だが、[[1851年]]（[[嘉永]]4年）に建替えが行われたことが確認できる。2018年（平成30年）11月に[[川崎市地域文化財]]（[[有形文化財]]）の決定を受けた<ref>[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html 第1回川崎市地域文化財を決定しました（川崎市教育委員会文化財課）]</ref> <ref>[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html 地域文化財紹介 高津区 橘樹神社社殿（川崎市教育委員会）]</ref>。
==周辺== 橘樹神社のある川崎市高津区子母口の高台は、[[東急田園都市線]]の[[梶が谷駅]]から続く「[[たちばなの散歩道]]」の終点にあたり、付近には[[縄文時代]]早期の[[標式遺跡]]である「[[子母口貝塚]]」や弟橘媛の[[墓|御陵]]とされる「富士見台古墳」（高津区子母口富士見台）、[[白鳳文化|白鳳]]の開基といわれる「[[川崎歴史ガイド#橘樹郡家と影向寺|影向寺]]」（[[宮前区]]野川）と、その塔頭十二坊の一つであった「能満寺」（高津区千年）などがあり、豊かな歴史的環境に恵まれた地域である。
また、[[江戸]]（東京）から続く[[中原街道]]が[[丸子橋|丸子の渡し]]で[[多摩川]]を越えた最初の[[台地]]にあたる場所で、ここから西へ続く[[多摩丘陵]]の入口でもある。
=== 交通 === [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[南武線]] *[[武蔵新城駅]]下車、徒歩約20分または路線バス利用。
[[川崎市バス]]（[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm 時刻案内]） *杉10 [[武蔵小杉駅|小杉駅]] - 子母口住宅前 - 蟹ヶ谷 *川64 [[川崎駅]]西口・労災病院前 - [[元住吉駅|元住吉（駅前）]] - 子母口住宅前 - 蟹ヶ谷
子母口住宅前で下車、徒歩約5分。なお案内標示等は用意されていない。
[[東急バス]]（[http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/ 時刻案内]） *溝22 [[溝の口駅]] - 子母口 - 蟹ヶ谷 *城01 [[武蔵新城駅|新城駅]] - 子母口 - [[綱島駅]]
子母口で下車、徒歩約5分。なお案内標示等は用意されていない。
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|橘樹神社}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 神奈川県神社庁 橘樹神社] *[http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ 川崎市高津区 橘樹神社（たちばな神社）ホームページ] *[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm ようこそ川崎へ - 川崎の魅力 - 市内の自然スポット - たちばなの散歩道]
{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=神奈川県}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:たちはなしんしや かわさきし}} [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:川崎市の神社]] [[Category:高津区の歴史]] [[Category:高津区の建築物]] [[Category:橘樹郡|社ちはなしんしや]] [[Category:総社 (地域)]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:弟橘媛]]

{{神社 | 名称=橘樹神社 | 画像=[[画像:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | 所在地=神奈川県川崎市高津区子母口122 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=日本武尊、弟橘媛 | 社格=村社 | 創建=不詳 | 例祭=10月8日 | 地図={{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=橘樹神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}

かつては[[多摩川]]沿いの[[田|田園]]風景の中にそびえる丘であった富士見台は、現在は宅地造成や道路敷設によって削られ、古墳頂上部の一部、高さ 3.7[[メートル|m]]、直径 17.5m の部分のみが姿を留めており、公園として管理されている。頂上に登れるよう綱が渡されているが、その頂上から見える眺望は近傍に並ぶ住宅に遮られている。
現在の古墳頂上からは眺望は広がっていないが、古墳がある丘陵地（富士見台）の西側は[[矢上川]]へ落ちる斜面となり、[[富士山]]を眺望でき、東側の斜面からは眼下に[[多摩川低地|多摩川の沖積低地]]が広がり（現在は住宅街が広がる）、子母口公園からは眺望が広がる。南側斜面からは[[蟹ヶ谷]]・[[井田山]]の丘陵の眺望が広がる（[[たちばなの散歩道]]）。
==祭神== *主祭神：[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]、[[弟橘媛]]
== 主な年中行事 == * 例祭：（10月8日）
==文化財== * 橘樹神社社殿：創建年代は不明だが、[[1851年]]（[[嘉永]]4年）に建替えが行われたことが確認できる。2018年（平成30年）11月に[[川崎市地域文化財]]（[[有形文化財]]）の決定を受けた<ref>[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html 第1回川崎市地域文化財を決定しました（川崎市教育委員会文化財課）]</ref> <ref>[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html 地域文化財紹介 高津区 橘樹神社社殿（川崎市教育委員会）]</ref>。
==周辺== 橘樹神社のある川崎市高津区子母口の高台は、[[東急田園都市線]]の[[梶が谷駅]]から続く「[[たちばなの散歩道]]」の終点にあたり、付近には[[縄文時代]]早期の[[標式遺跡]]である「[[子母口貝塚]]」や弟橘媛の[[墓|御陵]]とされる「富士見台古墳」（高津区子母口富士見台）、[[白鳳文化|白鳳]]の開基といわれる「[[川崎歴史ガイド#橘樹郡家と影向寺|影向寺]]」（[[宮前区]]野川）と、その塔頭十二坊の一つであった「能満寺」（高津区千年）などがあり、豊かな歴史的環境に恵まれた地域である。
また、[[江戸]]（東京）から続く[[中原街道]]が[[丸子橋|丸子の渡し]]で[[多摩川]]を越えた最初の[[台地]]にあたる場所で、ここから西へ続く[[多摩丘陵]]の入口でもある。
=== 交通 === [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[南武線]] *[[武蔵新城駅]]下車、徒歩約20分または路線バス利用。
[[川崎市バス]]（[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm 時刻案内]） *杉10 [[武蔵小杉駅|小杉駅]] - 子母口住宅前 - 蟹ヶ谷 *川64 [[川崎駅]]西口・労災病院前 - [[元住吉駅|元住吉（駅前）]] - 子母口住宅前 - 蟹ヶ谷
子母口住宅前で下車、徒歩約5分。なお案内標示等は用意されていない。
[[東急バス]]（[http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/ 時刻案内]） *溝22 [[溝の口駅]] - 子母口 - 蟹ヶ谷 *城01 [[武蔵新城駅|新城駅]] - 子母口 - [[綱島駅]]
子母口で下車、徒歩約5分。なお案内標示等は用意されていない。
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|橘樹神社}} *[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 神奈川県神社庁 橘樹神社] *[http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ 川崎市高津区 橘樹神社（たちばな神社）ホームページ] *[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm ようこそ川崎へ - 川崎の魅力 - 市内の自然スポット - たちばなの散歩道]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Yamato Takeru]]
[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_Rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakoro Waki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} * [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Washinomiya Shrine|Washinomiya Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] [[ja:橘樹神社 (川崎市)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:川崎市の神社]] [[Category:弟橘媛]] [[Category:橘樹郡]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧村社]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:高津区の歴史
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Yamato Takeru]]
[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_Rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Yamato Takeru]]
[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_Rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_Rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Yamato Takeru]]
[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_Rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi
