revision.comment: Merge [[Category:弟橘媛]] into [[Category:Ototachibana-hime]] via bot
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Yamato Takeru]]
[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_Rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakoro Waki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * (山武市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} * [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Washinomiya Shrine|Washinomiya Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]
[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_Rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 revision.comment: Merge [[Category:橘樹郡]] into [[Category:Tachibana District]] via bot
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = [[File:Tachibanajinjya_06c0859x.jpg|256px]] | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|34|21.85|N|139|37|33.81|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]
[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_Rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakoro Waki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine Shrine|ja|海南神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * (山武市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} * [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Washinomiya Shrine|Washinomiya Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11544300]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = File:Tachibanajinjya 06c0859x.jpg | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.57273611111111|N|139.62605833333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]
[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_Rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | prefecture= {{ill|Takatsu-ku|qid=Q1076736}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakoro Waki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11544300]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = File:Tachibanajinjya 06c0859x.jpg | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.57273611111111|N|139.62605833333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]
[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_Rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | prefecture= {{ill|Takatsu-ku|qid=Q1076736}} }} '''Tachibana Shrine''' (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan'''. It enshrines '''[[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]''' and '''[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]]'''. During the '''[[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]''', it was classified as a '''village shrine (村社)'''. Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the '''Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙)''' in ancient times, it is considered to have been the '''district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社)''' shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == '''History''' == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the '''guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village'''. It enshrines '''two deities in the form of male and female statues''', which are believed to be '''Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime'''. The shrine was also historically known as '''"Tachibana-sha" (立花社)'''. === '''Founding Legend''' === According to shrine tradition, '''when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea'''. His wife, '''Ototachibana-hime''', sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, '''her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location''', leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the '''record in the Kojiki''', which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, '''the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill''', is sometimes considered to be '''Ototachibana-hime's burial site'''. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === '''Fujimidai Kofun''' === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the '''Fujimidai Kofun''', which some believe to be the resting place of '''Ototachibana-hime'''. However, '''archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century''', making it more likely the tomb of a '''local ruler of the time'''[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In '''modern times''', the area around '''Fujimidai''' has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a '''residential area''', renaming it '''"Shimoguchi Fujimidai"'''. The ancient Kofun, once a '''prominent hill overlooking the Tama River''', has been partially leveled for development. Today, '''only a portion remains''', measuring '''3.7 meters in height''' and '''17.5 meters in diameter''', and it is maintained as a '''public park'''. == '''Deities Enshrined''' == * '''Main Deities:''' ** [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] == '''Annual Events''' == * '''Main Festival:''' October 8 == '''Cultural Heritage''' == * '''Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿)''' ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was '''rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4)'''. ** In '''November 2018''', the shrine was designated a '''Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property'''[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == '''Surrounding Area''' == Tachibana Shrine is located at the '''end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道)''', which extends from '''Kajigaya Station''' on the '''Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line'''. The area is rich in history, featuring: * '''Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚)''' – A Jomon-period site * '''Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳)''' – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * '''Yōgōji (影向寺)''' – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * '''Nōmanji (能満寺)''' – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands '''on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し)'''. This marks the entrance to the '''Tama Hills (多摩丘陵)'''. == '''Access''' == === '''By Train''' === * '''JR Nambu Line''': ** '''Musashi-Shinjo Station''' – 20-minute walk or bus ride === '''By Bus''' === * '''Kawasaki City Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** '''Sug10 Route''' – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakoro Waki Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakoro Waki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Jingu (Fujisawa City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} * [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Washinomiya Shrine|Washinomiya Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} }} {{shinto shrines}} {{translated page|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|version=101757650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{Sōja_shrines}} [[sn:Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)]] [[ja:橘樹神社 (川崎市)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kawasaki]] [[Category:Ototachibana-hime]] [[Category:Tachibana District]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] '''Kaw64 Route''' – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae''' – 5-minute walk * '''Tokyu Bus''' ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** '''Mizo22 Route''' – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** '''Shiro01 Route''' – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** '''Nearest Stop:''' '''Shimoguchi''' – 5-minute walk == '''References''' == {{Reflist}} == '''External Links''' == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * revision.text:
[[ja:橘樹神社 (川崎市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11544300]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = File:Tachibanajinjya 06c0859x.jpg | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.57273611111111|N|139.62605833333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]
[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_Rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | prefecture= {{ill|Takatsu-ku|qid=Q1076736}} }} Tachibana Shrine (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. It enshrines [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] and [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was classified as a village shrine (村社). Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙) in ancient times, it is considered to have been the district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社) shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village. It enshrines two deities in the form of male and female statues, which are believed to be Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime. The shrine was also historically known as "Tachibana-sha" (立花社). === Founding Legend === According to shrine tradition, when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea. His wife, Ototachibana-hime, sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location, leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the record in the Kojiki, which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill, is sometimes considered to be Ototachibana-hime's burial site. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === Fujimidai Kofun === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the Fujimidai Kofun, which some believe to be the resting place of Ototachibana-hime. However, archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century, making it more likely the tomb of a local ruler of the time[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In modern times, the area around Fujimidai has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a residential area, renaming it "Shimoguchi Fujimidai". The ancient Kofun, once a prominent hill overlooking the Tama River, has been partially leveled for development. Today, only a portion remains, measuring 3.7 meters in height and 17.5 meters in diameter, and it is maintained as a public park. == Deities Enshrined == * Main Deities: ** [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] == Annual Events == * Main Festival: October 8 == Cultural Heritage == * Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿) ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4). ** In November 2018, the shrine was designated a Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == Surrounding Area == Tachibana Shrine is located at the end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道), which extends from Kajigaya Station on the Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line. The area is rich in history, featuring: * Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚) – A Jomon-period site * Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳) – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * Yōgōji (影向寺) – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * Nōmanji (能満寺) – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し). This marks the entrance to the Tama Hills (多摩丘陵). == Access == === By Train === * JR Nambu Line: ** Musashi-Shinjo Station – 20-minute walk or bus ride === By Bus === * Kawasaki City Bus ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** Sug10 Route – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** Kaw64 Route – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** Nearest Stop: Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae – 5-minute walk * Tokyu Bus ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** Mizo22 Route – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** Shiro01 Route – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** Nearest Stop: Shimoguchi – 5-minute walk == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakorowake Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai-jingū|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai-jingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * (大東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * revision.text:
[[ja:橘樹神社 (川崎市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11544300]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = File:Tachibanajinjya 06c0859x.jpg | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.57273611111111|N|139.62605833333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]
[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_Rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | prefecture= {{ill|Takatsu-ku|WD=Q1076736}} }} Tachibana Shrine (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. It enshrines [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] and [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was classified as a village shrine (村社). Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙) in ancient times, it is considered to have been the district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社) shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village. It enshrines two deities in the form of male and female statues, which are believed to be Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime. The shrine was also historically known as "Tachibana-sha" (立花社). === Founding Legend === According to shrine tradition, when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea. His wife, Ototachibana-hime, sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location, leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the record in the Kojiki, which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill, is sometimes considered to be Ototachibana-hime's burial site. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === Fujimidai Kofun === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the Fujimidai Kofun, which some believe to be the resting place of Ototachibana-hime. However, archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century, making it more likely the tomb of a local ruler of the time[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In modern times, the area around Fujimidai has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a residential area, renaming it "Shimoguchi Fujimidai". The ancient Kofun, once a prominent hill overlooking the Tama River, has been partially leveled for development. Today, only a portion remains, measuring 3.7 meters in height and 17.5 meters in diameter, and it is maintained as a public park. == Deities Enshrined == * Main Deities: ** [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] == Annual Events == * Main Festival: October 8 == Cultural Heritage == * Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿) ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4). ** In November 2018, the shrine was designated a Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == Surrounding Area == Tachibana Shrine is located at the end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道), which extends from Kajigaya Station on the Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line. The area is rich in history, featuring: * Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚) – A Jomon-period site * Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳) – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * Yōgōji (影向寺) – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * Nōmanji (能満寺) – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し). This marks the entrance to the Tama Hills (多摩丘陵). == Access == === By Train === * JR Nambu Line: ** Musashi-Shinjo Station – 20-minute walk or bus ride === By Bus === * Kawasaki City Bus ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** Sug10 Route – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** Kaw64 Route – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** Nearest Stop: Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae – 5-minute walk * Tokyu Bus ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** Mizo22 Route – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** Shiro01 Route – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** Nearest Stop: Shimoguchi – 5-minute walk == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka (栗東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * revision.text:
[[ja:橘樹神社 (川崎市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11544300]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = deities of its local area | type = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]] | name = Tachibana Shrine | image = File:Tachibanajinjya 06c0859x.jpg | location = Shimoguchi 122, Takatsu Ward, [[:en:Kawasaki, Kanagawa|Kawasaki City]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.57273611111111|N|139.62605833333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]]
[[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] | shrine_Rank = [[Village shrine]] (村社) | founded = Unknown | annual_festival = October 8 | map = {{location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=34|lat_sec=21.85|lon_deg=139|lon_min=37|lon_sec=33.81|label=Tachibana Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | prefecture= {{ill|Takatsu-ku|WD=Q1076736}} }} Tachibana Shrine (橘樹神社, *Tachibana Jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in Shimoguchi, Takatsu Ward, Kawasaki City, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. It enshrines [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] and [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]]. During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was classified as a village shrine (村社). Due to its location in an area believed to have housed the Tachibana District Office (橘樹郡衙) in ancient times, it is considered to have been the district's [[Sōja shrine|sōja]] (総社) shrine[[Kawasaki Historical Guide#Tachibana District Office and Yōgōji|Kawasaki Historical Guide "Tachibana District Office and]][[Yōgōji]]", Kawasaki City Cultural Foundation, 2004 (Heisei 16). It enshrines many kami of the local region.It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a provincehttps://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ == History == Tachibana Shrine has long served as the guardian shrine of Shimoguchi Village. It enshrines two deities in the form of male and female statues, which are believed to be Yamato Takeru and Ototachibana-hime. The shrine was also historically known as "Tachibana-sha" (立花社). === Founding Legend === According to shrine tradition, when Yamato Takeru was on his eastern expedition, he encountered a great storm at sea. His wife, Ototachibana-hime, sacrificed herself by leaping into the waters to calm the storm. Eventually, her garments and headgear drifted ashore at this location, leading to the shrine’s foundation. This legend aligns with the record in the Kojiki, which states: > *"After seven days, the Empress's comb washed up on the shore. The Emperor took the comb and built a tomb to enshrine it."* Because of this connection, the Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳), located on a nearby hill, is sometimes considered to be Ototachibana-hime's burial site. However, no concrete historical evidence supports this claim[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000000069.html Tachibana Shrine and Fujimidai Kofun (Kawasaki City Board of Education)]. === Fujimidai Kofun === Behind the shrine, atop a hill, lies the Fujimidai Kofun, which some believe to be the resting place of Ototachibana-hime. However, archaeological findings suggest that the mound was built around the 6th century, making it more likely the tomb of a local ruler of the time[http://homepage2.nifty.com/plaza-k/name/ntaka/mizo13.htm Plaza Takatsu - Place Name Exploration - Fujimidai]. In modern times, the area around Fujimidai has been heavily urbanized. The city of Kawasaki developed the site into a residential area, renaming it "Shimoguchi Fujimidai". The ancient Kofun, once a prominent hill overlooking the Tama River, has been partially leveled for development. Today, only a portion remains, measuring 3.7 meters in height and 17.5 meters in diameter, and it is maintained as a public park. == Deities Enshrined == * Main Deities: ** [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] ** [[Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|Ototachibana-hime]] == Annual Events == * Main Festival: October 8 == Cultural Heritage == * Tachibana Shrine Main Hall (社殿) ** The exact date of its original construction is unknown, but records confirm that it was rebuilt in 1851 (Kaei 4). ** In November 2018, the shrine was designated a Kawasaki City Tangible Cultural Property[http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000101919.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - 1st Regional Cultural Property Designation][http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/880/page/0000120101.html Kawasaki City Board of Education - Regional Cultural Property Introduction]. == Surrounding Area == Tachibana Shrine is located at the end of the "Tachibana Walking Path" (たちばなの散歩道), which extends from Kajigaya Station on the Tokyu Den-en-toshi Line. The area is rich in history, featuring: * Shimoguchi Shell Mound (子母口貝塚) – A Jomon-period site * Fujimidai Kofun (富士見台古墳) – Associated with Ototachibana-hime * Yōgōji (影向寺) – A temple said to have been founded during the Hakuhō period * Nōmanji (能満寺) – A former sub-temple (塔頭) of Yōgōji Additionally, the shrine stands on the first plateau after crossing the Tama River via the Maruko Ferry (丸子の渡し). This marks the entrance to the Tama Hills (多摩丘陵). == Access == === By Train === * JR Nambu Line: ** Musashi-Shinjo Station – 20-minute walk or bus ride === By Bus === * Kawasaki City Bus ([Schedule Information](http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/82/82syomu/home/kotumenu.htm)) ** Sug10 Route – Musashi-Kosugi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** Kaw64 Route – Kawasaki Station → Motosumiyoshi Station → Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae → Kanigaya ** Nearest Stop: Shimoguchi Jutaku-mae – 5-minute walk * Tokyu Bus ([Schedule Information](http://www.tokyubus.co.jp/)) ** Mizo22 Route – Mizonokuchi Station → Shimoguchi → Kanigaya ** Shiro01 Route – Musashi-Shinjo Station → Shimoguchi → Tsunashima Station ** Nearest Stop: Shimoguchi – 5-minute walk == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tachibana-jinja, Kawasaki|Tachibana Shrine}} * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=53&cd=1201053 Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association - Tachibana Shrine] * [http://www.tachibana-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City, Takatsu Ward)] * [http://www.city.kawasaki.jp/30/30ryoku/home/sanpo/sanpo06.htm Kawasaki City - Tachibana Walking Path] {{Shinto-stub}} == Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one == * {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakorowake Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} * {{ill|Kōdai-jingū|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai-jingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Nagao Shrine|Nagao Shrine]] * {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one) * {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}} * [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Washinomiya Shrine|Washinomiya Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} }} {{shinto shrines}} {{translated page|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|version=101757650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{Sōja_shrines}} [[ja:橘樹神社 (川崎市)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kawasaki]]
